methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Rune Technologies is a Series A-stage Defense & Security company founded by David Tuttle and Peter Goldsborough, having raised $20M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private-stage firms in the same sector, including NODA AI, Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, and Saronic.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $20M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage advancement, yielding $40M. Peer-median analysis across 8 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables establishes a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $125M to $3.0B. The sampled peers—including Dream Security ($3.0B, Series B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), and Castelion and Defense Unicorns (both $1.0B)—reflect elevated valuations characteristic of the Defense & Security sector.

Triangulating these methods supports a valuation range of $34M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $520M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty regarding Rune's differentiation within the peer set and stage-appropriate risk adjustment. The peer-median method dominates the upper bound, while stage-progression anchors the floor. Resolution of technical capability claims and customer traction will be critical to narrowing this range.
